API Reference

Transactions API

Transaction lifecycle, commit/rollback, error handling, and patterns

#Transaction API

The Transaction API provides ACID-compliant transactions for consistent data operations.

#Creating Transactions

#Read-Only Transaction

#Read-Write Transaction

#With Options

#TransactionOptions

Property

Type

Default

Description

ReadOnly

bool

false

Read-only transaction

Timeout

TimeSpan

60s

Maximum duration

IsolationLevel

IsolationLevel

Snapshot

Isolation level

#Transaction Operations

#ITransaction Interface

#Get Container

#Query in Transaction

#Update in Transaction

#Commit and Rollback

#Explicit Commit

#Explicit Rollback

#Automatic Rollback

#Transaction States

#Error Handling

#Patterns

#Unit of Work

#Retry on Conflict

#Optimistic Locking

#Nested Operations

#Best Practices

  1. Keep transactions short - Minimize time between begin and commit

  2. Use read-only when possible - Better concurrency

  3. Don't hold transactions - Across async/await or user input

  4. Handle conflicts - Implement retry logic

  5. Always dispose - Use using statements

  6. Batch related operations - Single transaction for related changes

#Next Steps
